focus: Which cost and operator-friction defects dominate after route proof and unrestricted council seat startup are corrected?
noveltyJustification: Four new P1 defects show that default liveness, concurrency, timeout, and retry-budget controls do not contain their most expensive failure paths.

graphEvents.label: Council timeout can orphan process tree

label: Synthetic progress heartbeats prevent the lineage stall watchdog from detecting a hung executor
Council cost guards do not enforce upper caps or concurrency
Council seat timeout can leave a process tree alive after failure
Fan-out budget approval ignores the default six-attempt worst case
A liveness signal must derive from child progress rather than a parent timer
Cost approval must include concurrency dimensions and cumulative retry attempts
Host heartbeat resets child-silence watchdog timestamp
Council accepts arbitrary positive seat count and dispatches all seats concurrently
Seat timeout sends direct-child SIGTERM without process-group escalation
Lineage budget estimate excludes up to five retries

direction: Treat host-generated heartbeat rows as child executor progress
reason: The parent timer calls markLineageEvent without observing child output or state advancement.

direction: Treat computeCouncilCostUpperBound as a hard session cap
reason: The function only calculates metadata after accepting any positive dimensions.
